doc:
Pure-TS port of MPFR's mpfr_flags_clear (mpfr/src/exceptions.c L101-L107). Three-line C body: `__gmpfr_flags &= MPFR_FLAGS_ALL ^ mask` -- clear the bits named in `mask` from the global flag register.

The TS port follows the immutable-API contract used by the rest of the flag family (see mpfr_clear_divby0 / mpfr_clear_overflow etc): it takes (pre, mask) where pre is the prior global flag state and mask is the bits to clear, then returns the new flag state. This is the in-domain `pre & (MPFR_FLAGS_ALL ^ (mask & MPFR_FLAGS_ALL))` arithmetic the C performs.

Different from the targeted clear_divby0/clear_overflow family: this is the GENERAL mask-driven clear, where the caller chooses which bits to clear. Wire form takes both pre and mask.

Class 'misc' (1000ms per-case budget). Tag minimums per Rule 7 apply -- the input domain (pre in [0,63], mask in [0,63]) is small but enumerable.
divergence_from_c:
Three divergences:

  1. Return type: C returns void (mutates global state); TS returns bigint -- the new flag state after the clear operation.

  2. Parameter list: C takes (mask); TS takes (pre, mask) -- pre encodes the desired prior flag state. Matches the convention used by mpfr_divby0_p and the clear_<flag> family.

  3. Mechanism: The port computes `(pre & 63n) & (63n ^ (mask & 63n))` to mirror the C semantics with bit masking to the 6-bit domain. Equivalent to calling clearFlags(mask) from src/internal/mpfr/flags.ts after setFlags(pre).
refs: mpfr/src/exceptions.c L101-L107 -- the C reference body.
src/internal/mpfr/flags.ts -- the TS flag register module (already shipped); clearFlags(mask) is the canonical clear path.
